content.problemStatement:
Given an array consisting of **N** integers, you have to do the following operation **K** times.


Find out the sum of all the elements in the array. Denote it by **S**. Insert an integer in the array strictly greater than **S**. Hence, size of the array will increase by one. Thus, at the end of the **K** operations, the array will have **N** + **K** elements. Find out the smallest possible value of the last number inserted in the array. Since this number can be very large, you just have to tell whether it will be odd or even.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains N space‑separated integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing either "odd" or "even" – the parity of the last number inserted after performing the operation K times.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Initial sum = 12 (even). K >= 2, therefore the last inserted number is always even.
Initial sum = 6 (even). After the first insertion sum becomes 13 (odd). The second inserted number is 14 (even).
Initial sum = 10 (even). From the second operation onward the inserted numbers are even, so the last one is even.

solution.approach:
Let S0 be the sum of the initial array. To minimise the last inserted number we always insert the smallest possible integer, i.e. S+1 where S is the current sum. After an insertion the new sum becomes S' = S + (S+1) = 2*S + 1. Hence the recurrence is S_{i+1} = 2*S_i + 1. Modulo 2 this simplifies to S_{i+1} ≡ 1 (mod 2) for every i ≥ 0. Therefore:
- If K = 1, the last inserted number is S0 + 1, whose parity is opposite to the parity of S0.
- If K ≥ 2, the sum before the last insertion is already odd, so the last inserted number is odd + 1 = even.
Thus the answer can be obtained in O(N) time per test case by just computing the parity of the initial sum and checking whether K equals 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (reading the array).
